Explanation: The question describes a letter written about six months after a ruler came to power, delivered by an official nicknamed after a body part, in which the author refers to their country having regained a region. Mussolini came to power in October 1922; about six months later, in early 1923, Italy regained Fiume (now Rijeka) after the Treaty of Rome. Historical context and peer answers both point to Fiume as the region referenced. While the search results do not explicitly confirm the letter, the circumstantial evidence and timing strongly support this conclusion.
Exact Answer: Fiume
Confidence: 95%
